title: Reading into History: Immigrant Stories
tagline: Celebrate the stories of immigrants from around the world
description: On the final day of The New‑York Sari at the New‑York Historical Society, families can browse picture books and chapter books that highlight immigrant experiences. Kids ages 5–10 can try hands-on historical activities, enjoy a snack, and use a Family Guide to explore the exhibition. Free with museum admission, it’s a relaxed way for children to hear stories, see artifacts, and connect with different cultures.
description_source: Join us for the last day of The New York Sari exhibition when we celebrate the stories of immigrants from around the world. Discover the people and cultures that make up the unique blend of the American melting pot with a selection of chapter books and picture books. After browsing the books and enjoying some snacks, make sure to explore The New York Sari with a special exhibition Family Guide.

title: The New York Sari Sunday Family Fun Day
tagline: Celebrate the New York Sari exhibition with crafts and storytelling!
description: Celebrate The New York Sari at the New-York Historical Society with a family day of storytelling and hands-on textile crafts. Author Jyoti Tajan Gopal will read My Paati’s Saris, and kids (ages 3–12) can try print-making and learn how saris are worn across regions. Free with museum admission; pick up the Family Guide for an interactive tour.
description_source: Join us for a day of family programs celebrating The New York Sari exhibition! Meet special guest, author Jyoti Tajan Gopal, as we read her book 'My Paati's Saris.' Try your hand at print making with a special textile craft, and learn about the different ways saris are worn in different regions of the world. Discover every aspect of this exhibition with The New York Sari Family Guide, as well as special editions of Reading into History and Hablemos.

title: Hablemos: The New York Sari
tagline: Explore the cultural impact of the sari
description: Visit The New York Historical for Hablemos: The New York Sari, a guided tour that looks at how saris are made and what they mean in South Asian cultures. Kids ages 5–12 (must be with an adult) then stamp their own textile using patterns and colors from the show and enjoy a snack. Free with registration — a hands-on way for children to explore fabric, color, and stories.
description_source: New York City is a kaleidoscope of colors and patterns just like a sari. This traditional garment from South Asia is the protagonist of the exhibition 'The New York Sari'. In this edition of Hablemos, we’ll be looking at the cultural impact of this garment. Throughout the guided tour, we’ll learn about their creation, its use, and its meaning. Once you’ve been inspired by the saris in the exhibition, stamp your own sari taking inspiration from the patterns and colors in the exhibition.

title: Sunday Story Time: A Visit from My Paati’s Saris author Jyoti Rajan Gopal
tagline: Kick off New York Sari Family Day with a read aloud and sari wrapping demonstration
description: Author Jyoti Rajan Gopal reads My Paati’s Saris at The New York Historical, then demonstrates how to wrap a sari. Kids ages 2–10 will hear about the colors, folds and cultural meaning and get to design their own sari inspired by the illustrations. Admission $6–$24; children under 5 free.
description_source: Join author Jyoti Rajan Gopal for a read aloud and sari wrapping demonstration as part of New York Sari Family Day! In _My Paati’s Saris_, with illustrations by Art Twink, a young Tamil boy helps his grandmother to prepare for a celebration. Throughout the day, he takes comfort in the beautiful colors, folds, and patterns of Paati’s saris and delights in expressing his true self. After the story, design your own sari, inspired by the colors and patterns in the book!

title: Spring Fling 2026
tagline: Benefitting the Parrish Art Museum’s Education Programs
description: An evening of art, dance, music and food at the Parrish Art Museum benefiting its education programs. The night includes the 73rd annual Student Exhibition and marks the 10th anniversary of ACCESS PARRISH, which supports community members with neurodivergent, physical, and cognitive disabilities. Suitable for adults and supporters of local arts. Tickets start at $200 for members.
description_source: Join us for Spring Fling! An evening of art, dance, music, and food in support of the Parrish Art Museum’s educational programming. This year we celebrate the 10th anniversary of ACCESS PARRISH, created to support our year-round community members living with a range of neurodivergent, physical, and cognitive disabilities, promoting health and wellbeing, social connection, and curiosity. The event features the Museum’s 73rd annual Student Exhibition, showcasing the next generation of East End art...

title: Branches in Bloom: A Spring Festival Celebrating Arbor Day
tagline: A Spring Festival Celebrating Arbor Day
description: Two-day spring festival at Planting Fields Arboretum in Oyster Bay showcasing magnolias, flowering cherries and other spring blooms. Families—especially kids 5–10—can join guided tours, hands-on educational displays, live music and performances. Children can try tree climbing, explore nature up close and learn about trees while you grab a bite from food trucks. Entry is $30 per car.

title: Animal Care Trainee
tagline: Help care for APEC’s animals
description: Kids ages 8–10 spend a morning at Alley Pond Environmental Center learning to care for animal ambassadors through hands-on tasks like feeding, brushing, and cleaning. title: Orienteering Meet at West Hills County Park – North
tagline: Test your map reading skills in a fun scavenger hunt!

Registration is from 10:30 am-1 pm, and all competitors must finish by 3:30 pm.

Registration is FREE and open to participants ages 2–18 of all abilities. Participants will receive a New Balance souvenir (while supplies last).

title: Hopalong Andrew
tagline: A celebration of the multicultural history of the American West
description: Hopalong Andrew is a musical and dance program at Mott Haven Library that shares the multicultural history of the American West through songs, movement, and storytelling. Kids ages 5–12 will sing, try simple dance steps, and hear stories about cowboys and cowgirls from many backgrounds. Free admission.
description_source: This program honors the incredible-but-true multicultural history of the American West and celebrates the multicultural America of today, while recalling the empowering values associated with the iconic cowpoke: cowboys and cowgirls who possess traits such as a 'can do' spirit, resilience ('grit'), ingenuity, wry humor and wit. This program acknowledges the contributions to America’s Western heritage by peoples of all backgrounds.

title: Earth Month Intro to Composting
tagline: Learn the basics of composting with the Urban Park Rangers
description: Join the Urban Park Rangers at Blue Heron Nature Center to learn how composting works and how to keep a bin in a city home. Kids ages 5–12 will get hands-on making a bottle compost bin (bring a plastic bottle or use one provided). It’s a short, practical workshop where children can try sorting scraps and see how waste turns into soil. Free event.
description_source: Join the Urban Park Rangers and learn the basics of composting. You will learn how to start and maintain a compost bin as well as the different ways you can keep one in an urban setting. Then put those skills to the test and make your own bottle compost bin. Bring your own plastic bottle or they will be provided.

tagline: Get Ready for a Bloomin' Good Time!

title: Walk For Resilience 5K Run/Walk
tagline: A community-centered mental health event
description: This is a community 5K run/walk hosted by Carmel’s Depression & Anxiety Wellness Foundation to raise awareness and promote mental wellness. Families (ages 5–99) can walk, jog, or run at their own pace while kids get to move, hear a DJ, grab light snacks, and pose for free finish-line photos. Free to join at Prospect Park with timing, bibs, water, and live results.
description_source: The Walk for Resilience 5K Run/Walk is hosted by Carmel’s Depression & Anxiety Wellness Foundation (CDAWF). This inclusive 5K event brings together walkers, runners, families, and supporters to raise awareness for depression and anxiety while promoting movement, connection, and healing. Designed for all fitness levels, participants may walk, jog, or run at their own pace. The event emphasizes togetherness rather than competition, creating a supportive environment for mental health conversations.

title: International Public Policy Forum (IPPF) Finals Weekend
tagline: Compete for the grand prize and Brewer Cup
description: Watch the finals of the International Public Policy Forum, the culmination of a six‑month international debate series where high school teams argue global policy ideas. Finals weekend at NYU’s King Juan Carlos Center includes an educational symposium, live debates on a G20 education tax motion, and the $10,000 prize and Brewer Cup. Free with RSVP; best for students 15–18 who want to see policy debate and hear different viewpoints up close.
description_source: New York, March 18, 2026 — The Brewer Foundation is pleased to announce the 'Elite 8' teams in the 25th anniversary International Public Policy Forum (IPPF). The advancing teams earn an all-expenses-paid trip to New York City to compete in the IPPF Finals, where the IPPF World Champions will be awarded a $10,000 grand prize and the coveted Brewer Cup. Founded in 2001, the IPPF is jointly administered by the Brewer Foundation and New York University and is the world’s only international debate competition for high school students with a global policy focus.

address: 53 Washington Square S (Fl 3), New York, NY 10012
price_summary: Free admission to the symposium and finals, RSVP required

title: The Lion King
tagline: Experience the Circle of Life Live on Stage
description: The Lion King at the Minskoff Theatre is a stage adaptation of the Disney film with large-scale puppets, elaborate costumes and the original songs. Kids (recommended 6+) will follow Simba’s journey and watch the African savanna come alive on stage. They’ll experience live music, visual storytelling, and moments about friendship, courage and responsibility. All guests need tickets (from $111).
description_source: Join us for an unforgettable performance of 'The Lion King' at the Minskoff Theatre! title: South Street Seaport Museum Lenape Games
tagline: Discover Lenape culture through traditional games and interactive experiences.
description: Play traditional Lenape games like Selahtinalìtin (a straw-scattering game) and try hands-on activities led by instructors at the South Street Seaport Museum. Kids (recommended ages 5–12) will practice simple skills, take turns, and hear stories about Lenape traditions as they play. Open to all ages and free to attend.
description_source: Join us for an exciting day exploring the Lenape culture through games that were played by the Lenape people. This interactive experience is designed for families and children of all ages, and it provides a fun and educational way to learn about history. Participants will engage with hands-on activities that reflect the traditional games of Native Americans, ensuring a memorable and educational experience for everyone.

title: Hicks Nurseries’ 36th Annual Flower & Garden Show
tagline: Game of Gardens

Family-friendly activities include a scavenger hunt and a hands-on kids’ activity called Plant a Pea on weekends, allowing children to plant a seed to take home.

title: Cumbe Center for African Dance
tagline: An interactive experience celebrating African rhythms
description: At the Staten Island Children’s Museum, Cumbe Center leads an interactive African dance and music session for kids ages 5–12 (free with admission). Children try traditional instruments, learn lively steps, and hear the stories behind the rhythms. It’s hands-on movement that builds rhythm, teamwork, and cultural curiosity.
description_source: Feel the joy, energy, and heartbeat of rhythms rooted in African culture! In this interactive experience, kids won’t just listen – they’ll move and groove. Young participants will explore traditional African instruments, discover the stories behind the beats, and learn vibrant dances that celebrate community, creativity, and connection. Get ready to clap, stomp, and dance your way through an unforgettable cultural adventure!

external_id: Noble Maritime Collection: Sea Shanty Session
title: Sea Shanty Sessions
tagline: Join maritime music enthusiasts for a sing-along of authentic sea shanties in a historic setting.
description: Sing along to traditional maritime songs at the Noble Maritime Collection, led by the Folk Music Society of New York. Free, family-friendly sessions (third Sunday, 2–4 PM) honor sailor-song collector William Main Doerflinger. Kids can learn call-and-response shanties, try simple rhythms, and hear short stories about life at sea.
description_source: Led by the Folk Music Society of New York, this program offers a great opportunity to experience authentic, time-honored maritime songs in a historic setting. Sea Shanty Sessions are dedicated to William Main Doerflinger (1909-2000), the music scholar who collected traditional sailor’s songs from the residents of Sailor’s Snug Harbor. The sessions are free and family-friendly, and you are encouraged to sing along if the mood strikes you!

title: The Planter and the Plant – A Workshop Performance
tagline: A journey exploring the relationship between a planter and a plant.
description: A 30-minute theatre workshop-performance at QED Astoria for kids aged 3–8 and their families, free to attend. Children follow a planter and a seed as the plant grows, watching their relationship shift from curiosity to care and farewell. Short pre- and post-show activities let kids try ideas and share feedback while the piece is in development.
description_source: The Planter plants. Food, drinks, anything. One day, a seed grows into a plant. What follows is a journey exploring their evolving relationship: from getting to know the plant, to witnessing its unique growth, and finally, its departure. This is a theatre piece for 3–8 year olds and their families. This is a work-in-progress performance of a new theatre piece. We’re still in the exploration stage, and workshopping is the best way to gather audience feedback and discover what’s working, and what isn’t.

title: The Museum of Modern Art Presents Frida and Diego: The Last Dream
tagline: A first-of-its-kind collaboration with the Metropolitan Opera
description: See paintings, photos, and personal objects by Frida Kahlo and Diego Rivera at MoMA, part of a collaboration with the Metropolitan Opera. Kids (recommended 5–10) can explore colorful scenes and stories of the artists’ friendship and family life, sparking drawing ideas and questions. Free for NY residents on UNIQLO Friday Nights; other visits may need timed tickets.
description_source: The Museum of Modern Art announces an art exhibition, _Frida and Diego: The Last Dream._ _Frida and Diego: The Last Dream_ celebrates Frida Kahlo and Diego Rivera—two of Mexico’s most beloved icons of 20th-century art—in a first-of-its-kind collaboration with the Metropolitan Opera. Presented in conjunction with the Met’s new production of _El Último Sueño de Frida y Diego_ (May 14–June 5, 2026), composed by Gabriela Lena Frank with a libretto by Nilo Cruz, the exhibition features artworks by Kahlo and Rivera.

title: Face Value: Celebrity Press Photography
tagline: The first major exhibition of Hollywood studio portraiture
description: Face Value is a MoMA exhibition of over 200 Hollywood studio portraits from 1921–1996 drawn from the museum’s film stills archive. It’s a good pick for curious kids and adults—children can notice how photographers posed stars and how celebrity images changed before digital cameras and social media. Free entry for visitors 16 and younger.
description_source: The Museum of Modern Art announces *Face Value: Celebrity Press Photography*, the first major exhibition of Hollywood studio portraiture to be drawn from the Museum’s film stills archive since 1993. On view in the Titus and Morita Galleries, the exhibition will offer a revisionist look at the Department of Film’s photographic archive, examining the evolution of editorial practice before the digital age, AI technology, and social media reshaped the experience of celebrity.
price_summary: Free admission for all; general admission to MoMA is free for visitors 16 and younger.

title: The Orchid Show: Mr. Flower Fantastic’s Concrete Jungle
tagline: A dazzling reimagining of the Big Apple with orchids and concrete.
description: Thousands of orchids are arranged to recreate New York City scenes—stoops, subway stations and more—inside the Enid A. Haupt Conservatory. Kids (best for ages 5–12) can wander the floral city, spot details, and join hands-on programs to learn about orchid care and conservation. There are guided tours and Orchid Nights; members enter free, check the website for general admission.
description_source: Experience the breathtaking fusion of nature and cityscape artistry at The Orchid Show: Mr. Flower Fantastic’s Concrete Jungle. This exhibition showcases thousands of flowers in a stunning portrayal of New York City, from stoops to subway stations, at the Enid A. Haupt Conservatory. The event also features Orchid Nights with music and dining options, and programs to learn about orchid care and conservation.
price_summary: Free for members; general admission may apply. Check website for details.

title: ASL Slam at Lincoln Center
tagline: Join the celebration of American Sign Language and the Deaf/signing community
description: ASL Slam is a live showcase of American Sign Language poetry and storytelling at Lincoln Center’s David Rubenstein Atrium, part of the Big Umbrella Festival. Teens (13–18) will watch traditional, contemporary and experimental signed pieces, meet performers from the Deaf/signing community, and see how ASL uses rhythm, space and facial expression to tell stories. Free entry.
description_source: Having just celebrated their 20th anniversary in 2025, ASL Slam joins the Big Umbrella Festival at the David Rubenstein Atrium this April. The company’s mission is to preserve, nurture, showcase, and promote American Sign Language and the Deaf/signing community through performance by offering a stage for community members to share and create traditional, contemporary, and experimental sign language literature. ASL Slam provides a safe space for the Sign Language community to play with their language and art.

title: MainStage Concert: The Places That Inspire Us
tagline: Music inspired by real-world settings
description: At Our Saviour’s Atonement, WHCO presents Anna Clyne’s Restless Oceans alongside Samuel Barber’s Knoxville: Summer of 1915, with soprano Ariadne Greif. The program explores music that paints sea turbulence and childhood memories, so expect vivid, narrative-driven pieces. Good for listeners who enjoy emotionally rich classical music. Tickets $16.25–$21.50.
description_source: In The Places That Inspire Us, we explore music inspired by real-world settings and the emotional landscapes they evoke. The program begins with Anna Clyne’s Restless Oceans, a thrilling contemporary work for chamber orchestra that channels the turbulence and strength of the sea. This work draws inspiration and its title from A Woman Speaks – a poem by Audre Lorde. Next on the program, WHCO is joined by Ariadne Greif for Samuel Barber’s Knoxville: Summer of 1915, a nostalgic meditation on childhood...

title: Brooklyn Cyclones Bark in the Park
tagline: Bring your pooch to the park!
description: Bring your dog to Maimonides Park for a Brooklyn Cyclones game and a pre-game pup parade. Families with kids (0–12) can join dog-friendly activities like Kids Run the Bases, grab a Bark in the Park cap, and pick up giveaways. Up to two dogs per person and entry is free. title: Patterns at Lincoln Center
tagline: A multi-sensory dance performance by AXIS Dance Company for all ages, part of the Big Umbrella Festival
description: Patterns is a multi-sensory dance performance by AXIS Dance Company at Alice Tully Hall, part of the Big Umbrella Festival. D/deaf, neurodiverse, disabled and non-disabled dancers share live work and short interactive moments where kids can move along. Best for ages 5–18; pay-what-you-can (suggested $5).
description_source: Led by Artistic Director Nadia Adame and Executive Director Danae Rees, AXIS Dance Company is one of the nation’s most acclaimed ensembles of disabled, non-disabled, D/deaf, and neurodiverse performers. AXIS was founded in 1987 and is based in Berkeley, California, where the company creates world-class productions that challenge perceptions and redefine dance and disability. Alongside its artistic programming, the company provides unparalleled integrated dance education and outreach programs.

title: Family Concert: Music of Mozart
tagline: Explore the music of Mozart with your family!
description: Spend an afternoon at The Morgan with Young Concert Artists playing music by Mozart. Kids ages 5+ can listen, sing, and move along, then try hands-on artmaking and explore the Mozart exhibition. Tickets include museum admission; $30 adult/$10 child ($20 adult for members).
description_source: Visit the Morgan for an afternoon concert for our youngest visitors! This family-friendly performance with Young Concert Artists invites children to listen, sing, and move as they explore the music of Mozart. Pre- and post-concert activities include artmaking and a gallery exploration in the exhibition, Wolfgang Amadeus Mozart: Treasures from the Mozarteum Foundation of Salzburg. Recommended for families with children ages 5 and up. Families with children of all ages and abilities are welcome.

title: Two if by Sea: Lantern Workshop
tagline: Join us for a fun and illuminating craft workshop!
description: Join the Whaling Museum in Cold Spring Harbor on the anniversary of Paul Revere’s ride for a hands-on lantern workshop. Kids (ages 5–12) will hear how sailors used lanterns and how signals guided events during the Revolution, then make and decorate a decoupaged jar lantern. Suitable for young crafters; $8 members / $12 participants, and each child takes their lantern home.
description_source: Join us on the anniversary of Paul Revere's famous midnight ride for a fun and illuminating craft workshop! Discover how lanterns have been used to keep sailors safe at sea and to send secret signals during the American Revolution. Create a festive, decoupaged lantern jar to light up your home!

title: Antarctica! Crew Wanted
tagline: Crew wanted for a bold sensory expedition
description: Antarctica! Crew Wanted is an immersive sensory theatre for neurodivergent kids ages 6–10, inspired by Shackleton’s 1915 expedition. Through music, movement and hands-on sensory moments, children step into icy scenes, try teamwork tasks and explore survival ideas. At Samuel Rehearsal Studio; suggested $25.
description_source: Created for young people ages 6 and up who are neurodivergent, Antarctica! Crew Wanted is a theatrical experience that guides participants through an immersive, sensory-filled world, depicting the icy land of Antarctica. An Irish production created by sensory artists Phillida Eves and Amélie Bal, this theatrical performance with music and movement is inspired by Ernest Shackleton’s epic Antarctic expedition of 1915, when his ship Endurance was trapped in the ice and ultimately sank.

title: Downtown Brooklyn Car-Free Earth Day 2026
tagline: Join an afternoon of Earth-friendly and car-free activities
description: Spend an afternoon car-free at Albee Square with free family activities for kids 5–14. Children can learn to ride confidently with Bike New York, join soccer sessions with Brooklyn FC, try hand-sewing with Black Girls Sew and make art from reused materials; there's also e-waste drop-off and DJ music. Free.

Bike lessons by Bike New York for kids aged 5-14. Hand sewing with Black Girls Sew. Art projects from reused materials.

title: ARF’s Kitten Shower
tagline: The CUTEST baby shower of the year… is for kittens!
description: Bring kids to ARF’s Kitten Shower at the ARF Adoption Center in East Hampton to learn about caring for newborn kittens. Families (best for ages 5–12) can watch bottle‑feeding demos, try kitten‑themed crafts, and help gather supplies for foster kittens. It’s free and a hands‑on way for kids to see how volunteers care for and prepare kittens for adoption.
description_source: Kitten season is almost here, and ARF Hamptons is getting ready to welcome dozens of tiny, wiggly, purr-filled kittens. Every spring, vulnerable little ones arrive who need round-the-clock care—many are orphaned and rely on bottle feeding, warmth, and loving foster homes until they’re ready for adoption. You’re invited to celebrate at ARF’s annual Kitten Shower! Come help us gather supplies, learn how to care for bottle babies, and meet a few of our tiniest arrivals.

title: The Other Art Fair Brooklyn
tagline: Showcasing emerging artists and community activities
description: The Other Art Fair Brooklyn brings 130 emerging artists, hands-on installations, and family workshops at the Brooklyn Navy Yard. Kids ages 6–12 can try simple art projects, watch live illustration demos, and experiment with color and collage in guided activities that build creative confidence. Tickets $15–45.
description_source: In addition to showcasing 130 of the world’s most exciting emerging artists, The Other Art Fair Brooklyn presents a lively and inspiring program of features highlighting the richly diverse talents of the New York creative community.
address: 63 Flushing Ave, Building 92, Suite 112, Brooklyn, NY 11205

title: ASL Baby Slam at Lincoln Center
tagline: A family-friendly showcase promoting playful language acquisition through sign language.
description: ASL Baby Slam at the David Rubenstein Atrium is a free, family-friendly performance of American Sign Language as part of the Big Umbrella Festival. Kids can watch signed stories and join interactive language play to try simple signs, rhythm, and gestures. It's suited for babies, toddlers and caregivers who want a welcoming way to explore ASL and meet the Deaf community.
description_source: Having just celebrated their 20th anniversary in 2025, ASL Slam joins the Big Umbrella Festival at the David Rubenstein Atrium this April. The company’s mission is to preserve, nurture, showcase, and promote American Sign Language and the Deaf/signing community through performance by offering a stage for community members to share and create traditional, contemporary, and experimental sign language literature. ASL Slam provides a safe space for the Sign Language community to play with their lang...

title: Creatures of the Night: Bats
tagline: Explore NYC's Bats with Urban Park Rangers
description: Take an evening hike with Urban Park Rangers in High Rock Park to learn about the nine bat species that live in NYC. Kids ages 6–12 can look for signs of bat activity, hear how bats help local ecosystems, and ask questions along the trail. Free; a hands-on outdoor nature intro on Staten Island.
description_source: Did you know New York City is home to 9 species of bats? Join the Urban Park Rangers on an evening hike through High Rock Park to learn all about bats and their role in our ecosystem!

title: The Sound of Music (Singalong Version)
tagline: Join us for a musical journey!
description: Watch a singalong screening of The Sound of Music at Astoria’s Redstone Theater — the 1965 Rodgers & Hammerstein film in CinemaScope with Julie Andrews and Christopher Plummer. Kids (ages 3–17) can sing along to the songs, take part in audience participation, and enjoy the big-screen Salzburg scenery. Tickets: $10 youth; $17.50 general.
description_source: Join us for a singalong version of the beloved classic film 'The Sound of Music'! Directed by Robert Wise, this 1965 film features Julie Andrews and Christopher Plummer in an iconic adaptation of the Rodgers and Hammerstein musical. Experience unforgettable songs and stunning CinemaScope visuals shot in beautiful Salzburg, Austria. Perfect for kids and families!

title: Turtle Time
tagline: Explore the fascinating world of turtles!
description: Turtle Time is a 30-minute interactive session at Prospect Park Zoo where kids meet a live turtle up close. Children ages 5–10 and their caregivers will learn how turtles, tortoises, and terrapins differ, explore shell biology and survival adaptations, and hear a bit of their evolutionary story. Tickets are $10 ($8 members).
description_source: This fun, family-friendly program is all about turtles! In just 30 minutes, we’ll explore the fascinating differences between turtles, tortoises, and terrapins by meeting one of these amazing animals up close. Discover how these incredible reptiles survive in different habitats, what makes their shells so special, and why they’ve been around for millions of years. Perfect for curious kids and their caregivers, this engaging session will leave you turtally amazed!

title: Newsies JR.
tagline: A timeless story of rebellion, song, and spirit, based on the true 1899 newsboys' strike in New York City.
description: Disney’s Newsies JR. at The Secret Theatre is a 60-minute youth production of the Broadway musical about the 1899 newsboys’ strike. Packed with energetic song-and-dance numbers by Alan Menken and Jack Feldman, it follows Jack Kelly and the newsies as they organize against unfair price hikes. Great for kids 7–12 (recommended from 5), kids will enjoy lively performances and a simple lesson about standing up for fairness. Free admission.
description_source: Disney’s Newsies JR. is a 60-minute version of the 2012 Broadway musical, based on the 1992 film. Inspired by the rousing true story of newsboys in turn-of-the-century New York City, Newsies JR. features a Tony Award-winning score by Alan Menken and Jack Feldman and a book by Tony Award winner Harvey Fierstein. When powerful newspaper publishers raise prices at the newsboys’ expense, the charismatic Jack Kelly rallies newsies from across the city to strike against the unfair conditions. Together...

title: Ensemble Spotlight Series
tagline: Easter afternoon pageant of symphonic sounds and vocal treasures
description: Spend Easter afternoon at Stern Auditorium listening to youth orchestras and choirs from the Carolina Youth Symphony, Spring Valley Academy Band, and the Crystal Children’s Ensemble. Kids ages 3–12 will hear lively symphonic and choral pieces by Rutter, Whitacre and others, and see peers perform on a big stage. Family-friendly pacing and tickets from $46.
description_source: MidAmerica Productions presents an Easter afternoon pageant of symphonic sounds and vocal treasures. The Ensemble Spotlight Series features the return of the Carolina Youth Symphony, conducted by Charles Daniel Baldwin; the debut of the Spring Valley Academy Band, led by Donald Huff; and the Crystal Children’s Ensemble, led by Karl Chang, performing choral works by Rutter, Whitacre, Chilcott, Cao, Chou, and more.

title: Easter Sunday Egg Hunt
tagline: Hunt for wooden eggs and exchange them for prizes!
description: Drop in to Clay Pit Ponds Interpretive Center on April 5 between 11 AM and 2 PM for a self-guided, plastic-free Easter egg hunt. Kids ages 5–12 follow the nature trails to find wooden eggs and trade them for small prizes; children under 16 must be accompanied by an adult. Bring a basket — the event is free. Kids will practice observation skills while exploring the trails.

title: Fantastic Fungi Mushroom ID Hike
tagline: Explore the diversity of fungi in the forest!
description: Join a guided, hour-long mushroom ID hike at Reed's Basket Willow Swamp Park to learn how to spot and name local fungi. Kids ages 5–12 can handle some specimens, use ID sheets provided, and hear simple, kid-friendly facts about what mushrooms do in the forest. It's free and a hands-on way for families to explore nature together.
description_source: Prepare to be amazed by the wonders of the forest and the incredible diversity of fungi waiting to be discovered. Don't miss out on this opportunity to connect with nature, expand your knowledge, and make lasting memories. Join us for this adventure you won't want to miss!

title: Easter Parade and Easter Bonnet Festival
tagline: Join the festive celebration of Easter in New York City!
description: The Easter Parade on Fifth Avenue is a free, family-friendly pageant of people showing off bonnets as they march north from 49th to 57th Street, centered near St. Patrick’s Cathedral (about 10am–4pm). It’s a long-running city tradition celebrated in song by Irving Berlin. Kids will love the colorful hats and costumes — bring a bonnet if you want to join in.
description_source: Each year on Easter, celebrants don festive finery and show off their very best bonnets along Fifth Avenue. Immortalized by Irving Berlin (with some help from Judy Garland and Fred Astaire), the pageant is a New York City tradition that stretches back to the 1870s. Starting at about 10am and continuing until 4pm, the parade marches north on Fifth Avenue, from 49th Street to 57th Street. The best place to watch is from the area around St. Patrick's Cathedral; better yet, bring your bonnet and join the parade.

title: MOCACREATE: Celebrate Qing Ming & Welcome Spring with Teaching Artist Yu Rong
tagline: A workshop celebrating spring with art
description: Bring kids (ages 5–10) to the Museum of Chinese in America for a hands-on Qing Ming workshop with Teaching Artist Yu Rong. Children will sculpt clay tea eggs, oranges, cookies and figures to build a picnic scene, then make paper flower bouquets to remember loved ones. All materials provided; free to attend.
description_source: It’s time for the Qing Ming festival, when families remember those who’ve passed on and welcome spring. Families gather to honor their ancestors and make ritual offerings of food and flowers. Mold clay people, tea eggs, drum sticks, oranges, almond cookies, and more to create your very own Qing Ming picnic scene. Then create a paper flower bouquet in memory of those you love.

title: Easter Eggstravaganza at The Shoppes
tagline: Join the Shoppes for a fun-filled Easter celebration
description: Bring the kids to The Shoppes at East Wind for a free Easter Eggstravaganza with photos with the Easter Bunny (bring your camera or phone). The egg hunt starts at 1:00 PM and includes prizes, plus face painting, a bounce house and carousel rides. Kids ages 2–12 can hunt for eggs, try on face paint and enjoy active play. Free admission.
description_source: Join the Shoppes for a fun-filled Easter celebration the whole family will love! Bring the kids and get ready for a day of springtime excitement featuring photos with the Easter Bunny, a festive egg hunt with prizes, face painting, a bounce house, carousel rides and more. Photos with the Easter Bunny are free! A professional photographer will not be provided, so families are encouraged to bring their phones or cameras to capture their own photos. title: Recycled Ocean Crafts
tagline: Dive into the amazing wildlife of the Long Island Sound!
description: At the Whaling Museum & Education Center, kids (ages 5–18) can make sea‑creature sculptures from recycled materials in a self‑guided craft activity. Materials are provided and creations can be left to join the museum’s permanent "Recycled Ecosystem" display. Free with admission, it’s a hands‑on way to learn about Long Island Sound wildlife and recycling.

title: Urban Environmental Challenge Race
tagline: A fun trail race in the Bronx!
description: Join the Urban Environmental Challenge, a trail race through Van Cortlandt Park hosted by the Van Cortlandt Track Club. Open to teens 14–18 and adults, it’s a chance for teens to tackle real trail terrain, take in park scenery, and learn a bit about the local environment. Register online or on race day (8:30–9:30 a.m.); $35 advance, $40 day-of; small souvenir; rain or shine.
description_source: The Van Cortlandt Track Club (VCTC), proudly serving the Bronx since 1977, is excited to announce the Urban Environmental Challenge Race. This groundbreaking event marks the first true trail race in the five boroughs, set against the scenic backdrop of historic Van Cortlandt Park in the heart of the Bronx. Rain or shine, the race will proceed, showcasing the rugged terrain and natural beauty of the park. Participants can pre-register or sign up on race day (registration open from 8:30-9:30 a.m.).
venue_name: Parade Ground in Van Cortlandt Park
reviews: ['Great event for teens and young adults — challenging trail but well-organized. Souvenir was a nice touch.', 'My 16-year-old loved the race. The course was scenic and the volunteers were helpful. Would register again.', 'First trail race in the city? Yes! Clean start, good signage, and the park setting made it memorable. Only downside: limited spots.']

title: Sunday Morning Family Jams with Musiquita
tagline: A playful exploration of music in Spanish and English
description: Join Musiquita, a husband-and-wife duo, for a hands-on music session in Spanish and English at Learning Lab in Forest Hills. Kids ages 0–8 can sing, move, and try simple instruments—violin, ukulele, piano, melodica and percussion—while building rhythm, language skills and confidence alongside caregivers. Admission is $15 per person.
description_source: Musiquita is a playful exploration of music in Spanish and English, guided by a wife/husband duo, Blanca Cecilia González (vocals/violin/ukulele/percussion) and Jesse Elder (vocals/piano/melodica/percussion.) The couple performs a wide variety of music appropriate for all ages. Musiquita has two central goals: To uplift and encourage children and to strengthen and deepen the bonds between young people and the adults in their lives.
reviews: ['My 3-year-old loved the drums and singing along in both English and Spanish. We came back last month!', 'Perfect Sunday morning — no screens, just music and connection. The musicians are warm and engaging.', 'Wish the venue had more seating, but the energy was contagious. My 10-year-old danced the whole time.']

title: Girls Winter Softball Clinics
tagline: Learn and improve your softball skills this winter!

title: Seal Cruise
tagline: Join CRESLI scientists on a 2-hour expedition to view Atlantic harbor seals in Shinnecock Bay.
description: Spend two hours aboard the R/V Peconic with CRESLI scientists exploring Shinnecock Bay to spot and photograph Atlantic harbor seals. Kids 8+ can watch seal behavior up close, learn about the species and their recovery history, and even help collect simple data. The boat is open to the elements—dress warmly; registration and a $40 fee required.

title: 50 Years of Apple Computers: The Kevin Lenane Collection
tagline: Explore the history of Apple innovation
description: See a restored lineup of Apple machines—from a rare 1976 Apple‑1 to Lisa-era and early Macs—at the Long Island Museum in Stony Brook. Collector Kevin Lenane leads talks and demonstrations of vintage systems, sharing stories about design and tech changes. Kids (0–17) can watch demos and learn how personal computers evolved. Admission follows museum pricing; children under 6 are free.
description_source: Join collector Kevin Lenane for an engaging talk and guided tour of 50 Years of Apple Computers, an exhibition showcasing one of the most complete and meticulously restored Apple computer collections in the United States. As a fellow Long Islander, Lenane has assembled a remarkable collection that celebrates the technological and design innovations that brought personal computing from the desktop into everyday life. Learn about the evolution of Apple products, hear stories behind key pieces, and...

title: Video Games: The Great Connector
tagline: Exploring the impact of video games on youth and Long Island's role in gaming history.
description: At the Long Island Museum’s free exhibition, 10 hands-on gaming stations let kids play retro titles, try simple design challenges, and see how games build teamwork and problem-solving. The show highlights Long Island’s role — including Tennis for Two from Brookhaven in 1959 — and features contributions by people of color to game design. Best for ages 8–12, children can play, experiment, and meet stories of designers while parents join or watch.
description_source: On view in the LIM Art Museum from February 19 through May 24, 2026. This exhibition showcases how video games are more than just entertainment; they help develop personal and professional skills such as teamwork and problem-solving while also building social networks. With 10 interactive gaming stations, visitors will engage with games, meet influential figures in game design, and explore the art of gaming design, focusing on contributions by people of color to inspire Black and Latinx students. The exhibition also highlights Long Island’s role in gaming history, including 'Tennis for Two'—the first computer game, created at Brookhaven National Laboratory in 1959.
price_summary: Free admission for all; includes access to interactive gaming stations, retro games, and exhibition content.

title: The Museum of Modern Art Presents Frida and Diego: The Last Dream
tagline: Celebrating the legacy of Frida Kahlo and Diego Rivera
description: At MoMA, "Frida and Diego: The Last Dream" brings together works by Frida Kahlo and Diego Rivera alongside the Metropolitan Opera’s new production, showing how their art connects to music and theater. The installation’s bold colors, murals, and personal stories make it easy for kids to spot themes like identity and family. Free for ages 16 and under; adults $17–$30.
description_source: The Museum of Modern Art announces an art exhibition, _Frida and Diego: The Last Dream._ _Frida and Diego: The Last Dream_ celebrates Frida Kahlo and Diego Rivera—two of Mexico’s most beloved icons of 20th-century art—in a first-of-it-kind collaboration with the Metropolitan Opera. Presented in conjunction with the Met’s new production of _El Último Sueño de Frida y Diego_ (May 14–June 5, 2026), composed by Gabriela Lena Frank with a libretto by Nilo Cruz, the exhibition features artworks by Kahlo and Rivera.

description: Parallel Exit’s Sunset Circus is an immersive, family-friendly show at Marlene Meyerson JCC Manhattan featuring aerialists, jugglers, acrobats and comedians. Kids will be drawn into aerial moments and playful audience bits, and may get to try small tricks or clap along. Free and suitable for all ages, it’s a lively evening for the whole family.
description_source: Due to overwhelming success, Sunset Circus is BACK! Experience an immersive circus for audiences of all ages, presented by the physical theater company Parallel Exit. The extremely popular, New York Times-celebrated Sunset Circus features a company of 5-7 circus artists, including aerialists, jugglers, acrobats, and comedians. The sensational cast will entertain everyone, from kids to parents to grandparents, so bring the whole family!

title: The Little Mermaid The Musical
tagline: A magical tale of love and sacrifice beneath the sea.
description: A musical retelling of Hans Christian Andersen’s The Little Mermaid at The Players Theatre in NYC. The story follows a young mermaid who trades her voice to be with a human prince, brought to life with songs, costumes and playful sea scenes that keep kids focused. Best for ages 5–12; children will experience themes of choices and friendship and sing along. Tickets start at $34.
description_source: Once upon a time there was a little mermaid who dreamed of the day when she would finally be old enough to explore the wonders of the world above the sea. When her time comes, she is not disappointed. In fact she saves a young prince from drowning in a shipwreck and falls deeply in love with him. Now her longing increases, as she desires to become a mortal so she can marry her young prince; but as with all dreams, there are sacrifices. title: Visit a Brand-New Survivor Exhibit at The Paley Museum
tagline: Experience 50 seasons of Survivor!
description: See original props, costumes and artifacts from all 50 seasons of Survivor at The Paley Museum in New York through May 31, 2026. Kids 5–12 can explore immersive displays, spot iconic challenges and learn how the show is made; children under 12 enter free. Open Wed–Sun, 12–6; general tickets $16–$21.50.
description_source: In celebration of Survivor's landmark 50th season, The Paley Museum is presenting a dynamic, immersive exhibit honoring the iconic reality series now through May 31st. Featuring memorable props, iconic wardrobe pieces, and original artifacts spanning all fifty seasons, the exhibit offers fans a unique opportunity to relive Survivor’s most unforgettable moments and engage with the show in a whole new way. The Paley Museum is open every Wednesday – Sunday between 12 pm – 6 pm.

title: Jim Vines and Carl Mercurio announce Broadway Magic Hour March 2026 Shows
tagline: NYC's #1 Magic Show for All Ages
description: This 60-minute family magic and comedy show at Broadway Comedy Club mixes grand illusions, sleight-of-hand, mind-reading, and lots of audience participation. Kids (recommended ages 4–12) often get pulled on stage to try tricks and laugh along. Shows run select Fri–Sun in Times Square; tickets start at $20.
description_source: Laugh and be amazed as master magicians Jim Vines and Carl Mercurio take the stage in an all-new show featuring mind-boggling mysteries and hilarious comedy. The show runs on select Fridays, Saturdays and Sundays at the historic Broadway Comedy Club in NYC's theater district. This family magic show has it all – audience participation, comedy, grand illusion, mind-reading, and expert sleight-of-hand magic.

title: Aladdin & the Wonderful Lamp
tagline: A Tale from 1,001 Arabian Nights adapted for Marionettes by Nicolas Coppola
description: Puppetworks presents a fully staged marionette version of Aladdin, adapted from 1,001 Arabian Nights and best for ages 3–10. Kids will watch colorful puppets act out the adventure, magic, and humor, which can spark pretend play and storytelling. Shows Mar 28–29 and Apr 4, 6–7 at 12:30 & 2:30; reserve ahead. Tickets $11 kids, $12 adults.
description_source: Join us for a fully staged Marionette performance of 'Aladdin & the Wonderful Lamp', adapted for Marionettes by Nicolas Coppola. This enchanting tale is ideal for children aged 3 and up. Performances are set for March 28 & 29 and April 4, 6 & 7, 2026, at 12:30 & 2:30 p.m. Advance reservations are required as admission for walk-ins cannot be guaranteed.

title: Dead Father, Stepfather, Girl Father — A Comedy Show by A Chinese Queer Kid
tagline: A unique blend of comedy, drag, and political performance exploring queer identity and familial loss.
description: This solo show mixes stand-up, drag, burlesque and political theater as a Chinese queer performer digs into three decades of searching for father figures. Drawing on life in China, Taiwan and the U.S., it looks at grief, patriarchy and whether chosen family can replace biological ties. For ages 21+, tickets about $24–25 at Caveat.
description_source: It has been three decades since this only child from China began longing for approval and affection from multiple father figures—one deceased, one failed, and one who turns out to be more than just a father. After violence, grief, disappointment, and rebuilding, she/they begins to wonder: as a queer feminist, does one really need a father figure at all? title: Ice Queen Performance
tagline: A frosty adventure in Central Park
description: See a 45-minute musical at Buttenwieser Hall about a brave girl crossing a wintry Central Park to rescue her friend from the Ice Queen. Original songs, clear storytelling and snowy visuals keep kids ages 2–12 engaged. Children follow the action, learn about teamwork and friendship, and sing along. Tickets start at $32.
description_source: A young girl sets out across Central Park in a frosty gale to rescue her best friend from the clutches of an evil Queen. Through snow and ice and a slew of adventures, what follows is a timeless story — a very NYC retelling of the tale that inspired Disney’s Frozen — about the changing seasons of friendship that will warm the hearts of young audiences and their families alike. This musical is 45 minutes long and best enjoyed by audience members ages 2+.

title: Sunday Funday at Bronx Children's Museum
tagline: All the fun of a Saturday but on a Sunday
description: Every last Sunday of the month the Bronx Children's Museum hosts Sunday Funday, a hands-on morning of arts, crafts, exhibits and interactive play. Activities are geared to toddlers and preschoolers (ages 1–4), letting little ones explore textures, make simple crafts and try play-based learning. Admission: kids $8, adults $14, seniors $10; under 1 free.

title: Anish Kapoor: Mirror Works
tagline: Explore the reflective world of Anish Kapoor
description: At Lisson Gallery, Anish Kapoor’s mirror installations use polished stainless steel to bend light and reflections across large sculptures. Children (recommended 6–12) can watch themselves warp and shift in the surfaces, notice how space stretches, and compare different views up close. Free entry makes it an easy visit to spark questions about perception and light.
description_source: Anish Kapoor’s work transcends traditional boundaries, encouraging viewers to engage with the environment around them through reflective materials. The exhibition features large-scale installations and intimate sculptures that invite observers to reconsider their own presence and experience in the art world, using light, reflection, and form to challenge spatial perception.

title: Faith Ringgold: Artist, Storyteller, Activist
tagline: Exploring diversity through literacy and storytelling.
description: This exhibition at the Sugar Hill Children's Museum displays Faith Ringgold’s paintings, illustrated scenes from MLK Jr.’s Letter from Birmingham Jail, working drawings and children’s-book manuscripts. Kids ages 5–12 can explore storytelling and social justice through bold images and story-quilt ideas, seeing how an artist turns thoughts into books. Free admission makes it easy to bring the family.
description_source: Artist, writer, educator and activist Faith Ringgold was born in Harlem, not far from the Sugar Hill Museum. It was here that she had her first art studio, became an art teacher and began to raise her own family. Her love for the children of Harlem inspired Broadway Housing Communities to create the museum. The exhibition includes eight illustrated scenes from Martin Luther King, Jr.’s Letter From Birmingham City Jail, along with facsimiles of her working drawings and digital reproductions of ma... title: YOAH
tagline: Cirquework & Cluster Arts Global from Fukuoka, Japan
description: YOAH is a wordless contemporary circus from Japan mixing acrobatics, dance, traditional drumming and electronica with striking projections. Kids ages 7–12 will watch juggling, glowing diabolos and daring chair towers as a young wanderer meets Shinto spirit figures. At the New Victory Theater, children will feel the rhythm, see new circus skills up close, and leave inspired; tickets start at $25.
description_source: Established in 2021, Cirquework is a Japanese circus company founded by young circus artists active in Japan and other nations. Combining acrobatics, dance, music, lighting and visual effects, they create unique and innovative contemporary circus experiences for audiences. In the moonlit glow of dawn, a young wanderer named YOAH descends from her aerial silks into a circle of Shinto spirits, made manifest as mysterious maestros of cirque! Hypnotic projections play in sync with the performers as they send juggling balls soaring, conjure confidence atop a tower of chairs and fling their glowing diabolos to the heavens in hopeful arcs of neon. Set to a dramatic medley of traditional Japanese drumming and thumping electronica, this wordless symphony of circus will dazzle.

title: Hicks Nurseries’ 36th Annual Flower & Garden Show
tagline: Step into a world where board games meet blooms.
description: Take the kids to Hicks Nurseries’ 36th Annual Flower & Garden Show, a free indoor event in Westbury running March 5–29. The greenhouse has eight game-themed gardens with over 5,000 plants, perfect for little explorers and photo ops. Children (0–12) can do a scavenger hunt, join LEGO Make-and-Take workshops, and try planting peas to learn basic gardening.
description_source: February 4, 2026 – Westbury, NY — Hicks Nurseries, Long Island’s premier garden center, invites visitors to step into a world where beloved games meet breathtaking blooms at its 36th Annual Flower & Garden Show, Game of Gardens, running March 5–29, daily from 8am–6pm. The much-anticipated free indoor event transforms one of the Hicks Nurseries 20,000-square-foot greenhouses into a playful show inspired by the joy, creativity, and nostalgia of classic games. Guests will explore eight uniquely designed gardens and over 5,000 plants.
price_summary: Free admission; paid workshops available separately.

title: CAMP New York City
tagline: Immersive, themed play experiences for kids in the heart of NYC
description: CAMP NYC is an indoor play space in Manhattan with themed rooms like Bluey’s House, a Slime Bar, and hands-on craft stations for kids ages 3–10. Children can make slime, build crafts, and role-play in immersive sets, practicing creativity and fine motor skills while taking projects home. Admission is $30–$45; the venue is stroller- and wheelchair-accessible.
description_source: CAMP New York City provides interactive, themed play experiences for children aged 3–10, featuring immersive Bluey environments, messy slime-making, and creative craft stations. The venue is stroller and wheelchair accessible, with no advanced registration required for general admission. Additional paid experiences may be available.

title: Paint Your heART Out! Painting Class for Kids
tagline: Explore color and imagination through painting
description: A 60-minute painting class at The Art Studio NY for kids ages 6–10 that explores acrylic and watercolor techniques. Kids try layering, blending and composition while making several pieces inspired by famous artists and hearing fun art-history tidbits. All materials are provided, and the session focuses on play and skill-building. Free class (sold out).
description_source: In this class, young artists will discover the joy of painting through personalized instruction, learning techniques like layering, blending, and composition. They'll engage with art history fun-facts and create their own unique artworks inspired by famous artists. The class encourages self-expression and enhances skills such as focus and communication. All materials are included, making for a comprehensive and enjoyable learning experience.
